summary: Replayed ADR 0288 from clean latest-main source `58b38c6219c202f3b10766ae8fca5e87dfbeff51`, converged the private Crawl4AI runtime on `docker-runtime-lv3`, verified local health, monitor, markdown extraction, guest-network reachability from `coolify-lv3`, and the dedicated compose-managed `crawl4ai_runtime` bridge, then rebased the promotion onto the newer Piper-backed `origin/main` baseline, widened the Docker-chain startup recovery classifier after a real missing-iptables-chain failure on the live guest, and re-verified the final repository version `0.177.114` against platform version `0.130.75`.

verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml pytest -q tests/test_crawl4ai_runtime_role.py tests/test_crawl4ai_metadata.py tests/test_docker_runtime_role.py tests/test_common_docker_bridge_chains_helper.py tests/test_generate_platform_vars.py` returned `60 passed in 8.11s` on the rebased `0.177.114` tree, and the post-fix role regression `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml pytest -q tests/test_crawl4ai_runtime_role.py` returned `11 passed in 0.34s` after broadening the Docker-chain startup recovery classifier.
`make syntax-check-crawl4ai` passed on the synchronized final `0.177.114` tree before the successful rebased live replay.

verification.check: Authoritative live replay and rebased recovery replay
verification.observed: The first governed replay succeeded from committed source `58b38c6219c202f3b10766ae8fca5e87dfbeff51` with recap `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=107 changed=4 unreachable=0 failed=0 skipped=9 rescued=0 ignored=0`; after rebasing onto the Piper `0.177.113` mainline, a second replay exposed a missing Docker `DOCKER` iptables-chain startup failure, the role's rescue classifier was expanded to treat that message as recoverable drift, and `make converge-crawl4ai` then succeeded on the final `0.177.114` tree with recap `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=106 changed=4 unreachable=0 failed=0 skipped=10 rescued=0 ignored=0`.

verification.check: Independent local and guest-network runtime proofs
verification.observed: Independent SSH-driven probes returned healthy local `/health` and `/monitor/health` payloads on `docker-runtime-lv3`, a successful markdown extraction for `https://example.com/`, a healthy guest-to-guest `/health` response from `coolify-lv3`, and Docker inspection showed the `crawl4ai` container attached only to the dedicated `crawl4ai_runtime` bridge with host port `11235/tcp` published.

verification.check: Final repository automation and validation bundle
verification.observed: The final `0.177.114 / 0.130.75` tree passed `git diff --check`, `scripts/live_apply_receipts.py --validate`, `./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards workstream-surfaces generated-vars generated-docs health-probes`, full `make validate`, hosted `make remote-validate`, and hosted `make pre-push-gate`. During the replay, the branch repaired a Bash 3.2 empty-array expansion in `scripts/validate_repo.sh`, Python 3.9 callback compatibility in `scripts/mutation_audit.py` and the structured-log callback, the mirrored `release_tracks.platform_versioning.current` field, and the Crawl4AI startup failure classifier for Docker `DOCKER` iptables-chain drift before rerunning the full automation bundle to green.

notes: ADR 0288 is recorded as implemented in repository version `0.177.114` and first verified live in platform version `0.130.75`.
The compose-managed `crawl4ai_runtime` bridge is part of the success boundary because it removes reliance on Docker's legacy default `bridge` after the guest firewall refresh.

The validation-path repair is part of the committed surface for this workstream: `scripts/validate_repo.sh`, `scripts/mutation_audit.py`, the structured-log callback plugin, `tests/test_validate_repo_cache.py`, and the Crawl4AI runtime startup classifier were updated so the repository's own local and hosted automation gates can execute end to end on the current macOS Bash and Python 3.9/3.12 toolchains.

A failed rebased replay left orphaned Ansible Python workers holding the apt lists lock on `docker-runtime-lv3`; those transient wrappers were terminated manually, captured in the evidence bundle, and the governed playbook replay was then rerun successfully.
The workstream registry remains `ready_for_merge` on the workstream branch so branch-local ownership validation passes; the final fast-forward commit on `main` flips it to `merged`.
